reviews.review_description:
I grabbed this game, because, well, why not? In its current state, it's basically a workout game disguised as a story-based adventure. You chop projectiles with a sword, which somehow damages your alien enemies. You'll move a little, although I wish there was more dodging involved. I found that I barely had to move my body to dodge most of the projectiles. 

Another note to devs: I've only completed a few levels, but I've yet to play a level that involves a combination of moving/exploring AND the exercise-based combat. All the levels I've played so far feature only stationary combat and dodging. Graphics and presentation need a LOT of refinement and polish, but that's the easy part. I'm excited to see how this evolves!

l i n k t r . e e
/
q u a c k b i r t h

developer_response.body: This feedback is exactly what fuels our development! Thanks so much for taking the time to share it. In fact we're hard at work on a major update coming soon that addresses some of the issues you raised. Stay tuned, and we hope you'll consider revisiting the game when it launches! We'll keep you posted.

reviews.review_description: I really liked playing the game! The story is fun and each level adds different challenges and difficulties. The mechanics of the game make you move to win the battles. I also liked the ships and characters. And the level evolution reminds me of Mario and other platform games. I will be waiting to see what is next for this game and the continuation. But so far, so good. Definitely suggest getting the game of you’re fan of sci-fi and like story based games.
